strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: news/catalyst quality was clearly supportive|trend alignment was mixed rather than decisive|volume confirmation was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 67.5|Spread is wider than ideal but still usable.
decision_reasoning: MP shows the cleanest directional setup among candidates: +22% 20d, RS vs sector +0.23, volume 1.94x average, and sector/market tailwinds. However, news flow is sparse (no hard catalyst confirmation), IV is elevated (~69%) making premium expensive, and earnings_timing is 'unknown' which violates the comfort zone of PRD §17's BMO/AMC expiry rule — the 2026-05-15 expiry works only if timing is AMC or BMO on 5/08, unverified here. SWKS has weaker RS vs sector (-0.046), mixed trend signals, and a wider 15% spread on its best contract. CSCO and GH have no usable option chain. Net: MP is the single best setup to monitor but does not clear the recommend bar due to timing uncertainty and elevated IV into a binary event.
